title: Closed loop control of physiological glucose
abstract: The present disclosure relates to systems and methods for controlling physiological glucose concentrations in a patient.
claims: 1. A system for providing closed-loop control of physiological glucose, comprising: a drug delivery device configured to receive a first drug reservoir containing insulin and a second drug reservoir containing glucagon; a user interface configured to receive user input comprising exercise data; a glucose measuring device configured to measure a current interstitial glucose; and a controller, wherein in response to receipt of the exercise data, the controller is configured to estimate a physiological glucose based on the measured current interstitial glucose, calculate a corrected physiological glucose by subtracting an exercise factor from the estimated physiological glucose, and based on the calculation, to cause a glucagon bolus and an insulin bolus to be delivered in a closed-loop, wherein the glucagon bolus is deliverable only when a rate of change of the physiological glucose is less than a glucose rate threshold, and wherein the glucose rate threshold is a function of the estimated physiological glucose.
